name: Tomato Paste, Garlic
recipeInstructions:
To store tomato paste:
Most recipes for tomato-based pasta sauce call for a single Tablespoon of
tomato paste as a thickener. Here's a great way to keep what's left over.
Using a hand-held can opener, open both ends of the can and discard one.
Using the stem of a spoon as a plunger against the other end, push the paste
out in its original shape onto a piece of plastic wrap.
Score the paste into Tablespoon-sized pieces (most cans contain 6-8 Tablespoons).
Gently roll the plastic around the paste and freeze. When you need a single
Tablespoon, just cut off a frozen slice.
No-stick Garlic:
To keep garlic from sticking to your knife when chopping it, sprinkle 1/4 teaspoon
salt on the pieces as you go. The salt will absorb moisture from the garlic,
making it stick-free.
